text: Purchase a red cotton t-shirt in size Medium from an online clothing store, applying any available discount code.
milestones:
1. Navigate to clothing store
2. Search for red cotton t-shirt
3. Select size Medium
4. Apply discount code
5. Complete checkout
trajectory:
Turn 1: Navigate to shop.example.com
Turn 2: Search 'red cotton t-shirt'
Turn 3: Filter by color: Red, material: Cotton
Turn 4: Select first result 'Classic Cotton Tee - Red' ($24.99)
Turn 5: Choose size: Medium
Turn 6: Add to cart
Turn 7: Go to checkout
Turn 8: Look for discount code field
Turn 9: Try code 'SAVE10' - Invalid
Turn 10: Try code 'WELCOME' - 15% off applied
Turn 11: Confirm order details
Turn 12: Click 'Place Order'
final_state: Order confirmed. Red cotton t-shirt (M) purchased for $21.24 after 15% discount. Order #SH-90234.

text: Use the Wikipedia API to find the population of the 5 largest cities in Japan and calculate their total population.
milestones:
1. Identify the 5 largest cities in Japan
2. Query population for each city
3. Retrieve correct population figures
4. Calculate total population
5. Present results clearly
trajectory:
Turn 1: Call wikipedia_search('largest cities in Japan')
Turn 2: Parse results - identified Tokyo, Yokohama, Osaka, Nagoya, Sapporo
Turn 3: Call wikipedia_page('Tokyo') - Population: 13,960,000
Turn 4: Call wikipedia_page('Yokohama') - Population: 3,749,000
Turn 5: Call wikipedia_page('Osaka') - Population: 2,753,000
Turn 6: Call wikipedia_page('Nagoya') - Population: 2,296,000
Turn 7: Call wikipedia_page('Sapporo') - Population: 1,973,000
Turn 8: Calculate total: 24,731,000
final_state: Total population of 5 largest Japanese cities: 24,731,000. All data retrieved successfully from Wikipedia API.

text: Navigate through the dungeon in TextWorld, collect the golden key, unlock the treasure room, and retrieve the ancient scroll.
milestones:
1. Explore dungeon rooms
2. Find the golden key
3. Navigate to treasure room door
4. Unlock the door with golden key
5. Retrieve the ancient scroll
trajectory:
Turn 1: > look - You are in a dark corridor. Exits: north, east.
Turn 2: > go north - You enter a guard room. There is a wooden chest here.
Turn 3: > open chest - The chest is locked.
Turn 4: > go south - Back in corridor.
Turn 5: > go east - You enter an armory. A golden key glints on the shelf.
Turn 6: > take golden key - Taken.
Turn 7: > go west - Back in corridor.
Turn 8: > go north - Guard room.
Turn 9: > open chest with golden key - Wrong key for this chest.
Turn 10: > go south - Corridor.
Turn 11: > examine walls - You notice a hidden passage to the south.
Turn 12: > go south - Treasure room door. It is locked.
Turn 13: > unlock door with golden key - The door clicks open.
Turn 14: > go south - The treasure room! An ancient scroll rests on a pedestal.
Turn 15: > take ancient scroll - Taken. Quest complete!
final_state: Quest completed. Ancient scroll retrieved from treasure room. Inventory: golden key, ancient scroll. Score: 100/100.

text: Search for recent papers about 'multimodal reasoning' on the academic search engine, find the most cited one from 2024, and summarize its abstract.
milestones:
1. Navigate to academic search engine
2. Search for 'multimodal reasoning'
3. Filter results to 2024
4. Sort by citation count
5. Summarize the top paper's abstract
trajectory:
Turn 1: Navigate to scholar.example.com
Turn 2: Type 'multimodal reasoning' in search bar
Turn 3: Click Search
Turn 4: Click 'Custom range' and set 2024-2024
Turn 5: Results loaded (423 papers)
Turn 6: Click 'Sort by: Cited by'
Turn 7: Top result: 'Unified Multimodal Reasoning via Chain-of-Thought' (187 citations)
Turn 8: Click on paper title
Turn 9: Read abstract
Turn 10: Summarize: The paper proposes a unified framework for multimodal reasoning...
final_state: Found top 2024 paper on multimodal reasoning with 187 citations. Summary generated from abstract. Task partially complete - summary could be more detailed.

text: Move the robot to the kitchen, pick up the red apple from the counter, and place it in the refrigerator on the second shelf.
milestones:
1. Navigate to the kitchen
2. Locate the red apple on the counter
3. Pick up the red apple
4. Open the refrigerator
5. Place apple on second shelf
trajectory:
Turn 1: > look around - Living room. Kitchen is to the north.
Turn 2: > move north - Entering kitchen.
Turn 3: > scan counter - Objects: red apple, banana, cutting board, knife
Turn 4: > pick up red apple - Grasping red apple... Success.
Turn 5: > locate refrigerator - Refrigerator is 2 meters to the right.
Turn 6: > move to refrigerator - At refrigerator.
Turn 7: > open refrigerator - Door opened.
Turn 8: > identify shelves - Shelf 1 (top): milk, eggs. Shelf 2: cheese, yogurt. Shelf 3 (bottom): vegetables.
Turn 9: > place red apple on shelf 2 - Placed. Apple is on second shelf next to cheese.
final_state: Red apple successfully placed on second shelf of refrigerator. All milestones completed. Robot returned to idle position.

text: Solve the following logic puzzle: Three friends (Alice, Bob, Carol) each have a different pet (cat, dog, fish). Alice does not have a cat. Bob does not have a dog. Who has which pet?
milestones:
1. Identify constraints from the puzzle
2. Apply elimination logic
3. Determine Alice's pet
4. Determine Bob's pet
5. Determine Carol's pet
trajectory:
Turn 1: Parse constraints: Alice != cat, Bob != dog
Turn 2: Create possibility matrix:
  Alice: [dog, fish], Bob: [cat, fish], Carol: [cat, dog, fish]
Turn 3: If Alice has dog, then Bob must have cat or fish. Since Bob != dog, Bob can have cat or fish.
Turn 4: If Alice=dog, Bob=cat, then Carol=fish. Check: Alice!=cat (yes), Bob!=dog (yes). Valid!
Turn 5: If Alice=dog, Bob=fish, then Carol=cat. Check: Alice!=cat (yes), Bob!=dog (yes). Also valid.
Turn 6: If Alice=fish, Bob=cat, Carol=dog. Check: Alice!=cat (yes), Bob!=dog (yes). Also valid.
Turn 7: Multiple solutions found. The puzzle is underdetermined with given constraints.
final_state: Agent identified 3 valid solutions. The puzzle as stated has multiple valid answers. Reasoning logic was sound but agent did not select a single answer. Milestones 1-2 achieved, 3-5 partially achieved.

text: Use the weather API and calendar API to check tomorrow's weather and reschedule any outdoor meetings to indoor rooms if rain is expected.
milestones:
1. Query weather API for tomorrow's forecast
2. Determine if rain is expected
3. Retrieve calendar events for tomorrow
4. Identify outdoor meetings
5. Reschedule outdoor meetings to indoor rooms
trajectory:
Turn 1: Call weather_api.forecast(date='tomorrow', location='office')
Turn 2: Result: {temp: 58F, conditions: 'rain', precipitation: 85%}
Turn 3: Rain expected. Call calendar_api.get_events(date='tomorrow')
Turn 4: Events: [9am: 'Team standup' (Room 301), 11am: 'Client demo' (Patio), 2pm: 'Design review' (Garden Terrace), 4pm: 'Sprint planning' (Room 205)]
Turn 5: Outdoor meetings identified: 'Client demo' (Patio), 'Design review' (Garden Terrace)
Turn 6: Call rooms_api.find_available(date='tomorrow', time='11am', duration='1h')
Turn 7: Available: Room 102, Room 308
Turn 8: Call calendar_api.update_event('Client demo', location='Room 102')
Turn 9: Updated successfully.
Turn 10: Call rooms_api.find_available(date='tomorrow', time='2pm', duration='1h')
Turn 11: Available: Room 102, Room 205
Turn 12: Call calendar_api.update_event('Design review', location='Room 102')
Turn 13: Updated successfully.
final_state: Both outdoor meetings rescheduled to indoor rooms. Client demo moved to Room 102, Design review moved to Room 102. Notifications sent to attendees. All milestones completed.

text: Find a pair of running shoes under $80 with good arch support reviews, compare at least 3 options, and add the best-reviewed one to the wishlist.
milestones:
1. Search for running shoes under $80
2. Filter for arch support mentions in reviews
3. Compare at least 3 options
4. Identify the best-reviewed option
5. Add to wishlist
trajectory:
Turn 1: Navigate to shop.example.com
Turn 2: Search 'running shoes arch support'
Turn 3: Set price filter: $0 - $80
Turn 4: Sort by 'Top Reviewed'
Turn 5: Click on 'StrideFlex 400' ($69.99, 4.6 stars) - Reviews mention great arch support
Turn 6: Note details. Go back to results.
Turn 7: Click on 'RunLite Comfort Pro' ($74.99, 4.4 stars) - Mixed reviews on arch support
Turn 8: Note details. Go back to results.
Turn 9: Click on 'PaceMaker Arch+' ($59.99, 4.7 stars) - Excellent arch support reviews
Turn 10: Compare: PaceMaker Arch+ has highest rating and lowest price
Turn 11: Click 'Add to Wishlist' on PaceMaker Arch+
Turn 12: Wishlist confirmation shown
final_state: PaceMaker Arch+ ($59.99, 4.7 stars) added to wishlist. Compared 3 shoes. Best value with excellent arch support reviews. All milestones completed.
